Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple The attackers targeted the Ukrainian power grid, compromising multiple substation automation systems and causing widespread power outages.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, OS Process, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Code Injection, Process Hollowing, File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers sent spear phishing emails to healthcare employees, tricking them into revealing their login credentials.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers compromised the healthcare website using the BlackEnergy exploit, gaining access to the site's content management system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers injected malware into the compromised system, allowing them to execute arbitrary code.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-671: Requirements for ASIC Functionality Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attackers executed malicious code on the compromised system, further escalating their control.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attackers wiped data from the infected systems using the KillDisk malware.

Scenario.summary: The attackers compromised the healthcare website, injected malware into the system, executed malicious code, and finally destroyed data using the KillDisk malware.
